summary: Review failed: initiation-side project-context fallback is internally inconsistent and can send `demo-project` into the external review/embed path when no embed projectId is present, while the shared session resolver and tests use `debug-project`, leaving the baseline initiation context contract unstable for continued integration.

findings.description: `resolveProjectId()` falls back to `demo-project`, but the shared session resolver in `useUserStore.ts` and its tests standardize the default review project to `debug-project`. This creates two competing defaults for the same initiation context contract. In the common path where selected component names do not encode a project segment and embed mode omits `projectId`, the panel passes `demo-project` into `ExternalReviewViewer` and related embed requests, which can target a different backend project than the rest of the initiation/reviewer flow expects. The added tests only cover embed-project preference and store-side fallback, so this UI-side divergence is currently untested.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission artifacts and store tests treat `debug-project` as the canonical default review project, but `InitiateReviewPanel.vue` still hardcodes `demo-project` for its local project-context fallback.
sharedStateObservations.why: Scrutiny synthesis may need to decide whether the canonical fallback belongs in shared library/session logic and should be consumed by the initiation panel instead of duplicating an inconsistent literal.
